stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 2 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability between 73% and 85% (uncertain due to limited operational data). test 2 has a reliability of 79%. What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.9433 to 0.9685. Point estimate: 0.9559.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 tests fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.27 × 0.21 = 0.0567. P(works) = 0.9433.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.15 × 0.21 = 0.0315. P(works) = 0.9685.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
